summary: Northstar Logistics operations team spends 3-4 hours weekly consolidating regional manager updates from multiple formats (email, Notion, spreadsheets) into leadership briefings. Customer requests AI-assisted workflow to draft briefings with required fields (summary, risks, owners, next steps, open questions) starting with one region, with mandatory human review before posting to Notion.

stakeholders.name: Maya Chen
stakeholders.interests: Reduce time spent consolidating regional updates; ensure accurate action item ownership; maintain team trust in output quality

stakeholders.name: Regional managers
stakeholders.interests: Submit weekly updates (currently via email, Notion comments, and spreadsheets)

stakeholders.name: Operations lead
stakeholders.interests: Review and approve AI-drafted briefings before posting to Notion

risks.description: Operations team needs to trust AI-generated output quality before adopting the workflow
Action items may be assigned to wrong owners if extraction is inaccurate
Regional managers currently use inconsistent submission formats (email, Notion, spreadsheets), which may complicate data ingestion

next_steps.action: Create sample briefing workflow for one region

open_questions: Which region should be used for the pilot briefing?
What is the specific format/template for the leadership briefing in Notion?
Who is the operations lead that will perform human review?
How will regional manager inputs be ingested from multiple sources (email, Notion, spreadsheets)?
What approval workflow should be used in Notion before posting to leadership?
